summary: A vivid audience tape that captures the incendiary Scarlet > Fire stretch and a high‑energy second set, with clear sound and strong performances throughout.
review: This audience recording (AUD) delivers an immersive sense of being at the Worcester shows, especially the blazing Scarlet > Fire sequence that drives the momentum of the night. The band is in top form – Jerry’s lead work, Brent’s keyboards and Mickey’s drums all shine – while the tape remains surprisingly clean for a crowd source. Overall the show is praised as one of ’87’s spring‑tour highlights.

ai_rating.rationale: Average reviewer rating ≈ 4.6, overwhelmingly positive comments about both performance and tape quality; consistent praise across >15 reviews.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear crowd ambience that makes you feel present; generally clean with occasional tape hiss but no major drop‑outs or distortion.

standout_songs: Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain
show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set bursts from Scarlet straight into Fire and never lets up, creating a seamless high‑energy arc; first set is solid with strong openers (Cold Rain) and lively jams.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers praise Garcia’s guitar tone, lead lines on Scarlet/Fire and his vocal interaction with the crowd.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted as “clicking” with Jerry; praised for lyrical involvement on Gimme Some Lovin'.
band_member_comments.Bob: Fans highlight Bob’s vocal moments (Bobby Doubler) and enthusiasm on Good Lovin' and Around.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent is singled out for his “pokeyness,” exceptional Far From Me, and overall keyboard energy.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey’s drum duet praised as a standout moment; many call it one of the few audience‑tape drums worth listening to.
